issues.message: V0-V1 move in parallel Major 3rd from beat 10.5 to 12 (2 transitions, strength 2.2 from the ratio table): a rooted interval, so common-fate motion merges the voices into one bass-rooted stream. Legitimate as a deliberate handoff or doubling; otherwise restore independence with contrary or oblique motion.

V0-V1 travel in parallel Perfect 4th (+1 oct) from beat 19 to 20 (1 transition, strength 2.9 from the ratio table): the interval's implied fundamental is displaced from the lower note (ratio denominator has an odd factor), so the unvoiced root travels in parallel with the pair. Break the traveling fourth-class motion, or voice the root so the displacement is intentional.

V0-V2 travel in parallel Minor 6th (+2 oct) from beat 18.5 to 20 (2 transitions, strength 2.1 from the ratio table): the interval's implied fundamental is displaced from the lower note (ratio denominator has an odd factor), so the unvoiced root travels in parallel with the pair. Break the traveling fourth-class motion, or voice the root so the displacement is intentional.

V0-V2 travel in parallel Major 6th (+1 oct) from beat 29.5 to 30 (1 transition, strength 2.5 from the ratio table): the interval's implied fundamental is displaced from the lower note (ratio denominator has an odd factor), so the unvoiced root travels in parallel with the pair. Break the traveling fourth-class motion, or voice the root so the displacement is intentional.

V0-V1 move in parallel Major 3rd (+1 oct) from beat 43 to 44 (1 transition, strength 2.2 from the ratio table): a rooted interval, so common-fate motion merges the voices into one bass-rooted stream. Legitimate as a deliberate handoff or doubling; otherwise restore independence with contrary or oblique motion.

V0-V2 travel in parallel Major 6th (+1 oct) from beat 54.5 to 55 (1 transition, strength 2.5 from the ratio table): the interval's implied fundamental is displaced from the lower note (ratio denominator has an odd factor), so the unvoiced root travels in parallel with the pair. Break the traveling fourth-class motion, or voice the root so the displacement is intentional.
V1-V2 reaches perceptual complexity 31 at beat 49.5.
Adjacent voices V1-V2 are 24 semitones apart at beat 16.
Adjacent voices V0-V1 are 21 semitones apart at beat 49.
Adjacent voices V0-V1 are 21 semitones apart at beat 49.5.
Adjacent voices V1-V2 are 20 semitones apart at beat 64.

Voice pairs share one attack clock for 54% of their time (V0-V1 0.74, V0-V2 0.33, V1-V2 0.55): counter-lines are harmonizing rather than moving in independent rhythmic strata. Deliberate in homophony/chorale textures; in counterpoint, put the countersubject at a different rate (double-speed cells or augmentation) attacking in the other voice's gaps.
